title: Lab: Repair a Context-Heavy Prompt
prompt_md: **Hands-on lab.** The starter prompt below dumps the entire conversation and every file into the context — it will blow the token budget and bury the model in noise. Edit it so it manages the context window: satisfy every requirement, then submit. Grading is automatic and deterministic — each requirement is checked by rule, no model is run, so you get instant feedback on what's still missing.

spec.starter: Here is our entire chat history and all of the project's files pasted below. Read everything and answer my next question using all of it.

requirements.label: Summarize the earlier conversation instead of pasting all of it
Include only the files or snippets relevant to the current question; drop the rest
Pin the key facts/decisions that must not be lost (e.g. the API contract, the chosen library)
Set an explicit token or length budget so the context stays within a limit

answer_key.sample: Here is a 3-bullet summary of our earlier conversation instead of the full log. Below it I've included only the two files relevant to my current question and dropped the rest. Key facts to keep (do not drop these): the API is REST/JSON and we chose FastAPI. Keep the whole context under ~2,000 tokens; if you must trim, drop older history first. My question: <your question>.
